name: Christmas Eve Couscous
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Acorn squash (peeled, seeded, and diced)
recipeInstructions:
FOR THE STEW:
Melt the butter in a large saucepan. Add the onions and fry for 5 minutes. Add the carrots, squash, and spices. Cook with a lid on, stirring from time to time until all are buttery and spicy, about 10 minutes. Add water or stock and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until vegetables are tender. Add courgettes and broad beans, cooking for another 5 minutes. Season with salt, lemon juice, and sugar if necessary.
FOR THE GRAIN:
Put couscous into a baking dish and add 1 pint of water; drain off the liquid immediately. Return the couscous to the dish. Leave for 20 minutes, separating grains with fingers after 10 minutes. Place in sieve lined with cloth over simmering water (not required) for 20 minutes. Rinse grains with cold water mixed with salt and return to dish. Heat again for another 20 minutes.
ASSEMBLE AND SERVE:
Add a ladleful of stew liquid to harissa paste. Drain chick peas, heat oil, add cumin seeds, then chick peas. Toast pine nuts if desired. Add yogurt, dust with paprika. Serve stew over couscous and garnish with chopped fresh coriander.
